seoTitle: Gordon vs Gormanston — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gordon and Gormanston. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gordon and Gormanston.
heading: Gordon vs Gormanston — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gordon, Tasmania and Gormanston, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gordon has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Gormanston (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gordon or Gormanston safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gordon has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Gormanston scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gordon scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Gormanston scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gordon or Gormanston?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gordon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
